channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Abiy Ahmed dreams of remaking Ethiopia in his image
item.description: The prime minister’s re-election is certain. Ethiopia’s future is less so

item.title: China’s diplomatic successes are broad but shallow
item.description: It asks little of its foreign “partners”, and gives little back

item.title: Donald Trump says a deal with Iran is close. But he also says he is in no rush
item.description: Iran’s leaders are betting he needs an agreement more than they do

item.title: What an America-Iran deal might look like
item.description: Donald Trump says an agreement is close. But also that he is in no rush

item.title: Crackdowns on financial secrecy aren’t hurting offshore finance
item.description: There is a lot more to havens than crystal-clear waters and a promise of opacity

item.title: Offshore finance is thriving despite crackdowns
item.description: There is a lot more to havens than crystal-clear waters and a promise of opacity

item.title: Industrial Policy for an Age of Uncertainty
item.description: With industrial policy back in fashion worldwide, the question is not whether governments should intervene (they already do), but how they should proceed when they do not know in advance what will work. The answer lies in directed improvisation: creating the conditions for experimentation where it counts.

item.title: Is China’s Confidence Justified?
item.description: China’s political and intellectual elites increasingly see the country’s economic transformation as evidence that state-led development has succeeded where liberal capitalism has failed. Yet structural problems, including overcapacity and persistent inequality, suggest that triumphalism may be premature.

item.title: Why science is becoming less innovative
item.description: A huge study suggests ageing scientists are part of the problem

item.title: Starbucks Korea’s Tank Day Promotion on a Massacre Anniversary Causes a Political Firestorm
item.description: The marketing blunder on South Korea’s most solemn democratic anniversary has reignited a decades-old battle over how the country confronts its authoritarian past.

item.title: What to Make of Morocco’s Most Famous Anti-Colonial Hero?
item.description: A century later, the meaning of Abd el-Karim and the Rif Rebellion is still up for grabs.

item.title: The Policy Pivot Trump Needs
item.description: As November’s midterm elections approach, US President Donald Trump must stop pushing for a weaker dollar, which will exacerbate the affordability pressures many Americans face and harm the country’s fiscal position. Fortunately, there are several policy instruments that could help the administration support domestic manufacturing.

item.title: Europe Is Slowly Getting Ready to Ditch America
item.description: Trump’s bad bargains have shaken a complacent continent.

item.title: Whose Vote Will Count in America’s Midterms?
item.description: Democrats and Republicans alike are more focused on giving themselves an electoral advantage through redistricting than on appealing to voters, because both parties lack a credible vision for middle- and working-class Americans. Without an attractive program, all that is left are tactics.

item.title: How to Build a Sovereign AI Stack
item.description: Paradoxically, no economy can build the architecture of AI sovereignty alone. Coalitions of countries must work together to regulate the sector, design and operate the rails, open the model layer, and universalize the agent interface, thereby embedding contestability and interoperability into every layer.

item.title: The Political Truths of Literary Friendship
item.description: The private letters of famed literary critic Harold Bloom offer an ethical guide for politics.

item.title: The Philippines in 2026: Between Alliance Commitment and Strategic Hedging
item.description: Despite holding fast to its alliance with the United States, several recent developments suggest that Manila has not forgotten how to hedge.

item.title: Japan, Philippines to Begin Negotiations on Intelligence Sharing Agreement
item.description: A General Security of Military Information Agreement (GSOMIA) is expected to be high on the agenda when President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. visits Japan later this week.

item.title: ‘One man, one vote’? Maybe Not in Trump’s America
item.description: A recent Supreme Court decision threatens a core democratic principle.

item.title: The Middle Power Delusion
item.description: Not choosing is not an option.

item.title: Japan’s Point of No Return
item.description: Will Washington squander Tokyo’s new security commitment?

item.title: Vietnam Auctions Convicted Tycoon’s Luxury Car, Hermès Handbags For Over $1M
item.description: The property developer Truong My Lan is currently serving a life sentence for masterminding Vietnam's largest ever corruption scandal.
